title: parse_proxy_addr: credential URLs produce unusable host string
description: When a user passes socks5://user:pass@127.0.0.1:9050, parse_proxy_addr strips the scheme prefix and gets user:pass@127.0.0.1:9050. rsplit_once(':') correctly extracts port 9050, but the returned host string is "user:pass@127.0.0.1" — credentials included. This string is then passed to Socks5Stream::connect(("user:pass@127.0.0.1", 9050), ...). tokio-socks 0.5 does NOT support userinfo in the host string; it tries to parse the string as an IpAddr (fails), then treats it as a domain name and sends the literal "user:pass@127.0.0.1" string to the SOCKS5 proxy, which will reject it. The test proxy_addr_parses_port_ignores_credentials documents this as expected behavior ("tokio-socks will fail at connect time"), but the error the user sees is a cryptic SOCKS5 protocol error with no indication that credential URLs are unsupported. Fix: detect userinfo via rfind('@') and either strip it (if unauthenticated proxies only) or bail with a clear error message: "SOCKS5 proxy credentials in URL are not supported; configure proxy authentication separately".
